{"method":"item/completed","params":{"item":{"type":"agentMessage","id":"msg_05594c0eadf56d2e0169c4aa2e002c819aa7201dfce0cd6afb","text":"Here’s the current end-to-end flow I found.

### 1) Entry points / dialog mounting
- In the app wrapper, `excalidraw-app/App.tsx:1019-1021` mounts both `AIComponents` and `TTDDialogTrigger`.
- Clicking the trigger calls `setAppState({ openDialog: { name: "ttd", tab: "text-to-diagram" } })` in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDDialogTrigger.tsx:23-31`.
- `openDialog` is typed in `packages/excalidraw/types.ts:388-392`.
- `TTDDialog` itself lives in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDDialog.tsx:26-133`. It:
  - checks `appState.openDialog?.name === "ttd"` (`:36-42`)
  - lazy-loads `@excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw` (`:70-82`)
  - renders the text-to-diagram tab and the Mermaid tab (`:95-131`).

One subtlety: `LayerUI` has a fallback `<TTDDialog __fallback />` at `packages/excalidraw/components/LayerUI.tsx:483`, but the real app overrides it with the host-mounted `TTDDialog` from `AIComponents`; fallback suppression is handled by `withInternalFallback` in `packages/excalidraw/components/hoc/withInternalFallback.tsx:53-67`.

### 2) Core dialog state
The shared Jotai state is in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDContext.tsx:7-17`:
- `chatHistoryAtom`
- `rateLimitsAtom`
- `showPreviewAtom`
- `errorAtom`

Key types are in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/types.ts`:
- `LLMMessage` (`:11-14`)
- `TChat.ChatMessage` / `TChat.ChatHistory` (`:26-46`)
- `TTTDDialog.OnTextSubmitProps` / `OnTextSubmitRetValue` (`:84-106`)
- `TTDPersistenceAdapter` (`:62-72`)

### 3) UI submit flow -> chat history updates
`TextToDiagram` wires everything together in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TextToDiagram.tsx:50-80`.

The input box is in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/ChatInterface.tsx:73-86`:
- submit trims `currentPrompt`
- calls `onGenerate({ prompt })`
- clears `currentPrompt`

The main state machine is `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useTextGeneration.ts:66-223`:
1. validate prompt (`:42-64`)
2. abort any prior stream (`:74-81`)
3. normal flow:
   - append user message
   - append empty assistant placeholder  
   via `useChatAgent` (`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/useChatAgent.ts:11-32`)
4. repair/retry flow:
   - **mutates the last assistant message in place** instead of adding a new visible turn (`useTextGeneration.ts:83-96`)
5. build messages for the backend from chat history:
   - `getMessagesForLLM(chatHistory)` in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/chat.ts:77-92`
   - then only keeps the last 3 previous messages and appends the new user prompt (`useTextGeneration.ts:101-106`)
6. stream chunks into the last assistant message using `updateAssistantContent()` (`useTextGeneration.ts:122-129`, helper in `utils/chat.ts:5-33`)
7. mark `isGenerating: false` on finish (`useTextGeneration.ts:133-137`)
8. parse-check the final Mermaid with `parseMermaidToExcalidraw()` (`:193-203`)

Important contract detail: the UI content is built from `onChunk`, not from `generatedResponse`. So a custom `onTextSubmit` that only returns `{ generatedResponse }` but never calls `onChunk` won’t populate the assistant bubble.

### 4) Current app’s backend boundary
The current app injects `onTextSubmit` in `excalidraw-app/components/AI.tsx:104-122`. It uses `TTDStreamFetch()` against:

- `${VITE_APP_AI_BACKEND}/v1/ai/text-to-diagram/chat-streaming`

The client-side streaming/fetch code is `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/TTDStreamFetch.ts:84-225`:
- POSTs `{ messages }`
- expects SSE `data:` lines containing JSON chunks of type:
  - `"content"`
  - `"done"`
  - `"error"` (`:19-34`)
- accumulates `fullResponse`
- forwards deltas through `onChunk`
- extracts rate-limit headers (`:36-46`, `:111-113`)

The actual server prompt/model logic is **not in this repo**; the repo stops at this fetch boundary.

### 5) Final response -> preview rendering
The preview path watches the **last assistant message** in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useMermaidRenderer.ts:28-212`.

Key behavior:
- it throttles preview renders while streaming (`:18-21`, `:101-151`)
- it skips obviously incomplete Mermaid via `isValidMermaidSyntax()` (`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/mermaidValidation.ts:1-40`)
- it converts Mermaid -> Excalidraw preview data through `convertMermaidToExcalidraw()` (`useMermaidRenderer.ts:64-99`)

That helper lives in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/common.ts:43-131`:
- calls `parseMermaidToExcalidraw`
- converts returned elements via `convertToExcalidrawElements(..., { regenerateIds: true })` (`:97-105`)
- stores them into `data.current`
- renders preview canvas via `exportToCanvas()` (`:107-121`)

### 6) Final response -> actual scene updates
There are two insert paths:

#### A. Insert the currently previewed result
- `TextToDiagram.tsx:166-168` -> `insertToEditor({ app, data })`

#### B. Insert a historical assistant message directly
- `TextToDiagram.tsx:101-131`
- reconverts that message into a temp ref
- then calls `insertToEditor(...)`

`insertToEditor()` is in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/common.ts:139-169`:
- reads `data.current.elements/files`
- calls `app.addElementsFromPasteOrLibrary({ position: "center", fitToContent: true })` (`:159-164`)
- closes the dialog (`:165`)

The scene mutation is in `packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:3878-4025`:
- restores elements (`:3885-3887`)
- computes bounds / center (`:3888-3915`)
- duplicates and re-seeds them (`:3916-3925`)
- **appends** them to the current scene, not replace-only (`:3927-3955`)
- adds files (`:3973-3975`)
- selects inserted elements (`:3977-4012`)
- scrolls to fit content (`:4021-4025`)

So the AI result is turned into scene updates by:
`assistant Mermaid text -> common.ts convertMermaidToExcalidraw -> common.ts insertToEditor -> App.addElementsFromPasteOrLibrary`.

### 7) Chat persistence / restore
Persistence is pluggable via `TTDPersistenceAdapter` (`types.ts:62-72`).

Current app uses IndexedDB in `excalidraw-app/data/TTDStorage.ts:11-50`.

`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/useTTDChatStorage.ts:83-183`:
- autosaves when the last message stops generating (`:145-155`)
- stores only `user` and `assistant` messages (`:114-116`)
- caps saved chats to 10 (`:129-134`)

`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useChatManagement.ts:28-96`:
- restore converts timestamps back to `Date` (`:30-36`)
- resets `currentPrompt` to `""` on restore (`:38-42`)
- sets global error from the last assistant message (`:44-49`)
- deleting the active chat restores the most recent remaining chat or creates a new blank one (`:74-96`)

### 8) Tricky transitions / failure cases
The main ones I’d watch before changing this flow:

- **Repair/retry rewrites the last assistant message in place**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:83-96`, `TextToDiagram.tsx:133-164`  
  No new visible user turn is added for AI repair or retry.

- **Only the last 3 prior messages go to the backend**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:101-106`  
  The visible chat can be longer than the backend context.

- **Errored assistant content can still be sent back to the model**  
  `utils/chat.ts:77-92`  
  `getMessagesForLLM()` filters by type/content, not by `error`.

- **Abort leaves partial assistant content behind**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:133-180`  
  It clears `isGenerating` before checking for abort, so a stopped stream can remain as partial Mermaid with no error.

- **429 leaves the user prompt in history**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:143-169`  
  It removes only the assistant placeholder on 429, not the user turn.

- **Rate-limit warning dedupe looks wrong**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:149-167`  
  The filter keeps existing rate-limit warnings, then appends another one, so warnings can stack.

- **Delete on an assistant message truncates the conversation branch**  
  `TextToDiagram.tsx:170-184`  
  It slices to before the preceding user turn, not just that single message.

- **“Fix in Mermaid” uses localStorage as the handoff**  
  `TextToDiagram.tsx:82-99`, `common.ts:132-137`, `MermaidToExcalidraw.tsx:70-74`  
  It does not pass Mermaid text through shared chat state.

- **Preview conversion and final parse validation are slightly different code paths**  
  validation: `useTextGeneration.ts:193-203`  
  preview/insert conversion: `common.ts:73-95`  
  The preview helper has an extra quote-replacement retry that the validation step doesn’t.

- **Closing the dialog does not reset chat state**  
  `TTDDialog.tsx:85-89`  
  Reopening resumes the same `chatHistoryAtom` unless the user explicitly starts a new chat.

If you want, I can turn this into a compact call graph or annotate the exact functions you’d likely touch for a behavior change.","phase":"final_answer","memoryCitation":null},"threadId":"019d2831-d9bd-7c83-8d97-cccf91100c97","turnId":"019d2831-dae1-75a3-a13e-89e2ec51c24d"}}

The main state machine is `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useTextGeneration.ts:66-223`:
1. validate prompt (`:42-64`)
2. abort any prior stream (`:74-81`)
3. normal flow:
   - append user message
   - append empty assistant placeholder  
   via `useChatAgent` (`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/useChatAgent.ts:11-32`)
4. repair/retry flow:
   - **mutates the last assistant message in place** instead of adding a new visible turn (`useTextGeneration.ts:83-96`)
5. build messages for the backend from chat history:
   - `getMessagesForLLM(chatHistory)` in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/chat.ts:77-92`
   - then only keeps the last 3 previous messages and appends the new user prompt (`useTextGeneration.ts:101-106`)
6. stream chunks into the last assistant message using `updateAssistantContent()` (`useTextGeneration.ts:122-129`, helper in `utils/chat.ts:5-33`)
7. mark `isGenerating: false` on finish (`useTextGeneration.ts:133-137`)
8. parse-check the final Mermaid with `parseMermaidToExcalidraw()` (`:193-203`)

Important contract detail: the UI content is built from `onChunk`, not from `generatedResponse`. So a custom `onTextSubmit` that only returns `{ generatedResponse }` but never calls `onChunk` won’t populate the assistant bubble.

### 4) Current app’s backend boundary
The current app injects `onTextSubmit` in `excalidraw-app/components/AI.tsx:104-122`. It uses `TTDStreamFetch()` against:

- `${VITE_APP_AI_BACKEND}/v1/ai/text-to-diagram/chat-streaming`

The client-side streaming/fetch code is `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/TTDStreamFetch.ts:84-225`:
- POSTs `{ messages }`
- expects SSE `data:` lines containing JSON chunks of type:
  - `"content"`
  - `"done"`
  - `"error"` (`:19-34`)
- accumulates `fullResponse`
- forwards deltas through `onChunk`
- extracts rate-limit headers (`:36-46`, `:111-113`)

The actual server prompt/model logic is **not in this repo**; the repo stops at this fetch boundary.

### 5) Final response -> preview rendering
The preview path watches the **last assistant message** in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useMermaidRenderer.ts:28-212`.

Key behavior:
- it throttles preview renders while streaming (`:18-21`, `:101-151`)
- it skips obviously incomplete Mermaid via `isValidMermaidSyntax()` (`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/utils/mermaidValidation.ts:1-40`)
- it converts Mermaid -> Excalidraw preview data through `convertMermaidToExcalidraw()` (`useMermaidRenderer.ts:64-99`)

That helper lives in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/common.ts:43-131`:
- calls `parseMermaidToExcalidraw`
- converts returned elements via `convertToExcalidrawElements(..., { regenerateIds: true })` (`:97-105`)
- stores them into `data.current`
- renders preview canvas via `exportToCanvas()` (`:107-121`)

### 6) Final response -> actual scene updates
There are two insert paths:

#### A. Insert the currently previewed result
- `TextToDiagram.tsx:166-168` -> `insertToEditor({ app, data })`

#### B. Insert a historical assistant message directly
- `TextToDiagram.tsx:101-131`
- reconverts that message into a temp ref
- then calls `insertToEditor(...)`

`insertToEditor()` is in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/common.ts:139-169`:
- reads `data.current.elements/files`
- calls `app.addElementsFromPasteOrLibrary({ position: "center", fitToContent: true })` (`:159-164`)
- closes the dialog (`:165`)

The scene mutation is in `packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:3878-4025`:
- restores elements (`:3885-3887`)
- computes bounds / center (`:3888-3915`)
- duplicates and re-seeds them (`:3916-3925`)
- **appends** them to the current scene, not replace-only (`:3927-3955`)
- adds files (`:3973-3975`)
- selects inserted elements (`:3977-4012`)
- scrolls to fit content (`:4021-4025`)

So the AI result is turned into scene updates by:
`assistant Mermaid text -> common.ts convertMermaidToExcalidraw -> common.ts insertToEditor -> App.addElementsFromPasteOrLibrary`.

### 7) Chat persistence / restore
Persistence is pluggable via `TTDPersistenceAdapter` (`types.ts:62-72`).

Current app uses IndexedDB in `excalidraw-app/data/TTDStorage.ts:11-50`.

`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/useTTDChatStorage.ts:83-183`:
- autosaves when the last message stops generating (`:145-155`)
- stores only `user` and `assistant` messages (`:114-116`)
- caps saved chats to 10 (`:129-134`)

`packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useChatManagement.ts:28-96`:
- restore converts timestamps back to `Date` (`:30-36`)
- resets `currentPrompt` to `""` on restore (`:38-42`)
- sets global error from the last assistant message (`:44-49`)
- deleting the active chat restores the most recent remaining chat or creates a new blank one (`:74-96`)

### 8) Tricky transitions / failure cases
The main ones I’d watch before changing this flow:

- **Repair/retry rewrites the last assistant message in place**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:83-96`, `TextToDiagram.tsx:133-164`  
  No new visible user turn is added for AI repair or retry.

- **Only the last 3 prior messages go to the backend**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:101-106`  
  The visible chat can be longer than the backend context.

- **Errored assistant content can still be sent back to the model**  
  `utils/chat.ts:77-92`  
  `getMessagesForLLM()` filters by type/content, not by `error`.

- **Abort leaves partial assistant content behind**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:133-180`  
  It clears `isGenerating` before checking for abort, so a stopped stream can remain as partial Mermaid with no error.

- **429 leaves the user prompt in history**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:143-169`  
  It removes only the assistant placeholder on 429, not the user turn.

- **Rate-limit warning dedupe looks wrong**  
  `useTextGeneration.ts:149-167`  
  The filter keeps existing rate-limit warnings, then appends another one, so warnings can stack.

- **Delete on an assistant message truncates the conversation branch**  
  `TextToDiagram.tsx:170-184`  
  It slices to before the preceding user turn, not just that single message.

- **“Fix in Mermaid” uses localStorage as the handoff**  
  `TextToDiagram.tsx:82-99`, `common.ts:132-137`, `MermaidToExcalidraw.tsx:70-74`  
  It does not pass Mermaid text through shared chat state.

- **Preview conversion and final parse validation are slightly different code paths**  
  validation: `useTextGeneration.ts:193-203`  
  preview/insert conversion: `common.ts:73-95`  
  The preview helper has an extra quote-replacement retry that the validation step doesn’t.

- **Closing the dialog does not reset chat state**  
  `TTDDialog.tsx:85-89`  
  Reopening resumes the same `chatHistoryAtom` unless the user explicitly starts a new chat.

If you want, I can turn this into a compact call graph or annotate the exact functions you’d likely touch for a behavior change.

item.text:
Safest **public** extension point: the `TTDDialog` render props.

- Defined in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/types.ts:108-124`
  - `renderWelcomeScreen`
  - `renderWarning`
- Passed through in `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDDialog.tsx:116-121`
- Used here:
  - welcome screen: `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/ChatInterface.tsx:113-119`
  - warning messages: `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/ChatMessage.tsx:65-106`

Why this is safest:
- it only changes rendering
- it does **not** touch chat state updates, Mermaid parsing, preview generation, or scene insertion

If you need a bit more UI customization inside the repo, the next-safest layer is the **presentational components**:

- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/TTDChatPanel.tsx`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/ChatInterface.tsx`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/Chat/ChatMessage.tsx`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDPreviewPanel.tsx`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/TTDDialogPanel.tsx`

Those mostly render props/callbacks coming from higher-level orchestration.

Files I’d avoid if your goal is “UI only”:
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useTextGeneration.ts`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/hooks/useMermaidRenderer.ts`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/TTDDialog/common.ts`
- `packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx:3878-4025` (`addElementsFromPasteOrLibrary`)

So: **best answer = use `TTDDialog`’s render props first; if that’s not enough, limit edits to `Chat/*` + panel components and leave the hooks/common scene pipeline alone.**
